# Themes

> Paneflow's theme system with hot-reload from paneflow.json, no restart needed. One Dark ships today; the light theme returns after a redesign.

Canonical: https://paneflow.dev/docs/themes

<!-- Auto-generated mirror of paneflow.dev — do not edit by hand.
     Source of truth: the paneflow.dev docs. Regenerated by
     scripts/sync-public-docs.ts in the site repo. -->

Themes are set with the `"theme"` key in `paneflow.json` — the running
window hot-reloads within \~300 ms with no restart. Paneflow ships one
bundled theme today: `One Dark`, the dark default. `PaneFlow Light`
was pulled from the bundled set in v0.4.0 (2026-06-10) pending a full
light-theme redesign; a config still naming it falls back to `One Dark`
with a log warning. Themes drive every terminal-surface colour (the
24-colour ANSI palette, cursor, selection, scrollbar, links); the
sidebar and title bar stay on Paneflow's app chrome palette.

  **TL;DR.** v0.4.x bundles one theme, `One Dark`, active by default.
  A `"theme"` value naming anything else (including the retired
  `"PaneFlow Light"`) falls back to `One Dark` and logs a warning.
  Theme and typography keys hot-reload from `paneflow.json` within
  \~300 ms.

## How do I switch the active theme? [#how-do-i-switch-the-active-theme]

Set the `theme` key in `paneflow.json`:

```json
{
  "theme": "One Dark"
}
```

Prefer a UI? The **Appearance** tab in [Settings](/docs/settings) switches the theme and fonts with the same hot-reload:

The value must match one of the bundled theme names exactly,
including capitalisation. Unknown names fall back to `One Dark` and
emit a log warning the next time the file is parsed. The full set
of recognised keys lives in the [configuration schema](/docs/configuration/schema).

The config file lives at:

| OS    | Path                                                   |
| ----- | ------------------------------------------------------ |
| Linux | `~/.config/paneflow/paneflow.json`                     |
| macOS | `~/Library/Application Support/paneflow/paneflow.json` |

## Which themes ship with Paneflow today? [#which-themes-ship-with-paneflow-today]

| Name         | Description                                               |
| ------------ | --------------------------------------------------------- |
| `"One Dark"` | Dark theme, default. Inspired by Atom's One Dark palette. |

A Paneflow theme defines 36 colour slots: a 24-colour ANSI palette
(8 hues × 3 intensities — normal, bright, dim), 5 base
background/foreground colours, cursor, selection plus a derived
selection foreground, scrollbar thumb, link text, and 2 title-bar
colours. The selection foreground is not hand-tuned: Paneflow
recomputes it at every theme load until it clears APCA Lc ≥ 45
against the selection background, so selected text stays legible on
any palette.

  Earlier project plans referenced six bundled themes. Reality at
  v0.4.x is one: `PaneFlow Light` shipped through v0.3.x and was pulled
  in v0.4.0 pending a light-theme redesign. Additional bundled themes
  (the redesigned light theme, Solarized, Gruvbox, and a high-contrast
  variant) are on the roadmap; track [GitHub releases](https://github.com/ArthurDEV44/paneflow/releases)
  for the announcement.

## How does theme hot-reload work? [#how-does-theme-hot-reload-work]

When you save `paneflow.json`, Paneflow re-resolves the theme on the
fly - no restart, no window reload.

Two mechanisms drive the reload:

* **Event-driven (preferred).** A `notify` watcher installed on the
  config directory fires within milliseconds of a save. Events are
  debounced by 300 ms so editor save bursts (write -> fsync ->
  atomic-rename) only trigger one reload.
* **Polling fallback.** Filesystems that do not support inotify /
  FSEvents / ReadDirectoryChangesW (NFS shares, locked-down sandboxes,
  some WSL paths) fall back to a 500 ms `mtime` poll. The fallback
  only runs when the event watcher fails to start.

You will see the new palette take effect on the next render frame
after the watcher fires - typically within \~300 ms of the save. If
the reload silently fails on your machine, the [troubleshooting
page](/docs/troubleshooting#why-is-my-theme-not-hot-reloading) walks
through the three known causes (NFS share, sandboxed editor, atomic-
rename buffer larger than 300 ms).

  Hot-reload covers every theme-related field on `paneflow.json`,
  including the typography keys (`font_family`, `font_size`,
  `line_height`). The `window_decorations` key is the one config field
  that is read once at startup - changing it requires restarting
  Paneflow.

## How do I create a custom theme? [#how-do-i-create-a-custom-theme]

Custom user-supplied themes are on the roadmap. v0.4.x recognises
only the bundled theme name listed above; there is no `theme.json`
or custom palette format yet.

If you want a specific palette in the meantime, the most direct path
is to open a [GitHub issue](https://github.com/ArthurDEV44/paneflow/issues)
with the palette you want. Bundled themes are tuned in
`src-app/src/theme/builtin.rs` and ship with each release - new
palettes get fast-tracked when they have a clear use case.

The schema-level placeholder for a future custom-theme key is
deliberately not yet defined, so adding one to `paneflow.json` today
has no effect - it is silently ignored by the lenient runtime per
the [configuration overview](/docs/configuration#how-does-paneflow-handle-unknown-keys).
